bead.title: bench-pr-A: bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + --preset benchmark fix
bead.description:
First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL. Write final_status=interrupted to in-flight
    cells (process_outcome=killed, no metrics). Exit non-zero.
  - Concurrent-cells state file at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json. Runner appends its cell at
    start under flock, reads current count, writes into cell, removes at close.
    Prune stale entries (PID dead) at next read.
  - Budget: compute cost_usd_at_run_time per cell from profile.pricing × token streams
    at close. Accumulate into <out>/budget.json. Halt before next cell when cap hit.
    Skipped cells get final_status=budget_halted placeholder with process_outcome=
    setup_failed and a 'note' field.
  - Per-cell retry: bounded exponential backoff for transient errors (connection
    refused, 5xx, EOF parse). Write attempt_of: link in the new cell; back-write
    superseded_by: into the prior cell (the one allowed mutation of a closed cell).

SCOPE — harbor-runner Docker image:
  - scripts/benchmark/Dockerfile.harbor-runner:
      FROM python:3.12-slim
      RUN pip install --no-cache-dir harbor-bench==<pinned-version>
      COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/ /app/harbor_adapters/
      COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py /app/harbor_agent.py
      ENV PYTHONPATH=/app
      WORKDIR /app
      ENTRYPOINT [\"harbor\"]
  - scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh: docker build with image-content-sha
    label computed from sha256 of harbor_adapters/ + harbor_agent.py +
    Dockerfile.harbor-runner. ./benchmark preflight triggers rebuild if cached image
    label doesn't match current sha.
  - Pre-PR-A spike (5 min): confirm `pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>` works. If
    Harbor is git-only, use `pip install git+https://github.com/laude-institute/...`.

SCOPE — shell adapters (8 harness-adapters + 1 task-executor):
  - scripts/benchmark/task-executors/harbor: bash wrapper around
      docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \\
        -v \"\$cell_dir:/output\" -v \"\$tasks_dir:/tasks:ro\" \\
        -e FIZEAU_API_KEY -e FIZEAU_BASE_URL -e FIZEAU_MODEL ... \\
        fizeau-harbor-runner:latest run \\
        --agent-import-path \"\$harbor_plugin\" --task-id \"\$task_id\" --output /output ...
    Reads task-spec.json from stdin (see CONTRACT.md). Writes cell_dir/result.json.
    Captures task_executor_version + harbor_runner_image_digest once per ./benchmark
    invocation into <out>/sweep.json; runner copies into each cell.
  - scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/{fiz, claude, codex, opencode, pi, cost-probe,
    noop, dumb-script}: bash ports of scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/*.py. Each
    is executable with two subcommands and a '# SUMMARY:' header line.
      <name> install <agent-artifact-path> -> install-spec.json on stdout with
        {install_command, artifact_source, binary_path, harbor_plugin} where
        harbor_plugin names the Python class Harbor loads (e.g.
        'scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.fiz:FizeauAgent').
      <name> command (reads profile JSON on stdin) -> command-spec.json on stdout
        with {command, env, secret_env_keys}.
    The current Python adapters at scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/{fiz,opencode,
    pi,cost_probe,noop,dumb_script}.py are the reference for what each emits.
    For claude/codex: today they're invoked via cmd/bench/matrix.go:1015-1025
    passing --agent-import-path; the shell adapters emit the equivalent install-spec.
  - scripts/benchmark/runtime-probe.sh: case-on profile.metadata.runtime, curl + jq
    against each runtime's HTTP endpoint, emit model_server JSON (name, version,
    commit, endpoint) for the cell schema. Handles backends: lucebox, llamacpp, vllm,
    omlx, ds4, rapid-mlx (one case arm each).
  - scripts/benchmark/task-executors/CONTRACT.md and harness-adapters/CONTRACT.md
    document the JSON shapes and the '# SUMMARY:' header convention.

SCOPE — agent leak fix (Go change):
  - internal/serviceimpl/execute_native.go:265 today reads:
      PlanningMode: req.PlanningMode || req.ToolPreset == \"benchmark\",
    Delete the `|| req.ToolPreset == \"benchmark\"` clause.
  - Add field `sampling.planning_mode: bool` (default false) to profile YAML schema.
    harness-adapters/fiz reads it and adds `--plan` to the command when true.
  - Pre-PR-A spike: `rg -l 'preset.*benchmark|FIZEAU_PRESET' scripts/benchmark/profiles/`
    to find profiles relying on the preset; add `sampling.planning_mode: true` to each.

SCOPE — Go runner deactivation:
  - Modify cmd/bench/main.go to short-circuit execution subcommands (matrix, sweep,
    run, plan) with a one-line redirect 'use ./benchmark — see scripts/benchmark/README.md'
    and exit 2. Listing subcommands (profiles, bench-sets) stay functional until PR F.

SCOPE — parity fixture capture (must happen BEFORE deactivation merges):
  - Before opening PR A's deactivation commit: run the Go runner against canary
    (sindri-lucebox + tb-2-1-canary, 3 reps). Commit 3 representative cell reports
    to scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner/.
  - During PR A: run the bash runner against the same canary. Commit cells to
    scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner/.
  - scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/diff.sh: bash + jq tool that reports field
    divergence outside an allowlist (cell-id, timestamps, paths, NEW fields like
    harbor_runner_image_digest/model_server.commit/etc.).

OUT OF SCOPE (file separate beads):
  - cell-report.schema.json (PR B)
  - tasks_from / task_executor bench-set schema (PR C)
  - Node collector (PR D)
  - Node importers (PR E)
  - deleting cmd/bench (PR F)
  - docs (PR G)

ROLLBACK: If interrupted, leave no half-owned state. cell-state.json sentinels
written by partial runs are fine — they're the recovery anchor. The runner script
itself is one file; partial commits get reverted.

ACCEPTANCE — all of these commands pass on a clean checkout:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --plan --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     prints the matrix, creates NO files, builds NO Docker images.
  2. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     runs the canary and produces cells under bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/cells/.
     Each cell has report.json with embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt,
     fiz.err, session/.
  3. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     --max-cost-usd 0.01 produces at least one cell with final_status=budget_halted.
  4. scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/diff.sh against committed go-runner fixtures
     is clean per allowlist.
  5. SIGTERM to a mid-sweep ./benchmark: in-flight cells marked final_status=
     interrupted, harbor-runner containers stopped via docker stop, runner exits
     non-zero. Verify with: ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary
     & sleep 5; kill -TERM \$! ; wait; jq .final_status bench/results/.../report.json
  6. fiz-bench matrix exits 2 with redirect message:
     fiz-bench matrix 2>&1 | grep -q 'use ./benchmark'; test \$? -eq 0
  7. grep -n 'ToolPreset == \"benchmark\"' internal/serviceimpl/execute_native.go
     returns nothing.
bead.acceptance: All 7 commands in the description's ACCEPTANCE block pass.
